tool.name: ProSeg
tool.description: probabilistic cell segmentation for spatial transcriptomics
tool.command:
## INPUT PREPARATION
#if $input_mode.mode == "table"
#if $input_mode.transcripts.ext == "csv" ln -s '$input_mode.transcripts' input_data.csv && #else cp '$input_mode.transcripts' input_data.csv && #end if
#end if
proseg
## INPUT MODE
#if $input_mode.mode == "anndata"
--anndata
#elif $input_mode.mode == "zarr"
--zarr
#end if
## PLATFORM
#if $input_mode.mode == "table"
#if $input_mode.platform.preset == "xenium" --xenium #elif $input_mode.platform.preset == "cosmx" --cosmx #elif $input_mode.platform.preset == "cosmx_micron" --cosmx-micron #elif $input_mode.platform.preset == "merscope" --merscope #elif $input_mode.platform.preset == "visiumhd" --visiumhd #elif $input_mode.platform.preset == "manual" --gene-column '$input_mode.platform.gene_col' --x-column '$input_mode.platform.x_col' --y-column '$input_mode.platform.y_col' --z-column '$input_mode.platform.z_col' --cell-id-column '$input_mode.platform.cell_id_col' --cell-id-unassigned '$input_mode.platform.unassigned_id' #end if
#elif $input_mode.mode == "anndata"
#if $input_mode.platform.preset == "xenium" --xenium #elif $input_mode.platform.preset == "cosmx" --cosmx #elif $input_mode.platform.preset == "cosmx_micron" --cosmx-micron #elif $input_mode.platform.preset == "merscope" --merscope #elif $input_mode.platform.preset == "visiumhd" --visiumhd #end if
#elif $input_mode.mode == "zarr"
#if $input_mode.platform.preset == "xenium" --xenium #elif $input_mode.platform.preset == "cosmx" --cosmx #elif $input_mode.platform.preset == "cosmx_micron" --cosmx-micron #elif $input_mode.platform.preset == "merscope" --merscope #elif $input_mode.platform.preset == "visiumhd" --visiumhd #end if
#end if
## QUALITY FILTERING
#if $input_mode.mode == "table" #if $input_mode.qv_col and $min_qv --qv-column '$input_mode.qv_col' --min-qv $min_qv #end if #end if
## CELLPOSE PRIOR
#if $cellpose_masks --cellpose-masks '$cellpose_masks' #end if
#if $cellpose_probs --cellpose-cellprobs '$cellpose_probs' #end if
#if $cellpose_scale --cellpose-scale $cellpose_scale #end if
## VISIUMHD
#if $spaceranger_map --spaceranger-barcode-mappings '$spaceranger_map' #end if
## Z-AXIS
$ignore_z
## MORPHOLOGY
#if $tissue.mode == "annular" --cell-compactness 0.04 --enforce-connectivity #elif $tissue.mode == "custom" --cell-compactness $tissue.compactness $tissue.connectivity #else --cell-compactness 0.04 #end if
## CORE PARAMETERS
--voxel-size $voxel_size --burnin-voxel-size $burnin_voxel_size --voxel-layers $voxel_layers --samples $samples --burnin-samples $burnin_samples --recorded-samples $advanced.recorded_samples
## ADVANCED
--morphology-steps-per-iter $advanced.morph_steps --ncomponents $advanced.ncomponents --diffusion-probability $advanced.diffusion_prob --nuclear-reassignment-prob $advanced.nuclear_reassign $advanced.no_diffusion
#if $advanced.diff_sigma_near --diffusion-sigma-near $advanced.diff_sigma_near #end if
#if $advanced.diff_sigma_far --diffusion-sigma-far $advanced.diff_sigma_far #end if
#if $advanced.diff_sigma_z --diffusion-sigma-z $advanced.diff_sigma_z #end if
## OUTPUTS
--output-spatialdata proseg-output.zarr
#if $output_counts --output-counts counts.csv #end if
#if $output_expected --output-expected-counts expected.csv #end if
#if $output_cellmeta --output-cell-metadata cellmeta.csv #end if
#if $output_txmeta --output-transcript-metadata txmeta.csv #end if
#if $output_gene --output-gene-metadata gene.csv #end if
#if $generate_polygons --output-cell-polygons polygons.geojson #end if
#if $polygon_layers --output-cell-polygon-layers layers.geojson #end if
## INPUT FILE
#if $input_mode.mode == "table" input_data.csv #elif $input_mode.mode == "anndata" '$input_mode.anndata_input' #else '$input_mode.zarr_input' #end if
2> proseg_error.log || { cat proseg_error.log; exit 1; }
&&
tar -cf '$output_spatialdata' proseg-output.zarr
&&
echo "ProSeg run complete." > '$run_summary'

param.name: transcripts
param.label: Transcript table
param.help: CSV, CSV.GZ, or Parquet file of transcript positions with prior cell assignments.

param.name: gene_col
param.label: Gene column
param.help: Column containing the gene/feature name.

param.name: cell_id_col
param.label: Cell ID column
param.help: Column with prior cell assignments.

param.name: unassigned_id
param.label: Unassigned cell ID value
param.help: Sentinel value indicating an unassigned transcript.

validator.message: Only alphanumeric characters, underscores, and hyphens are allowed.

param.name: qv_col
param.label: Quality value (QV) column
param.help: Column containing per-transcript quality scores (e.g. Xenium qv field).

param.name: cellpose_masks
param.label: Cellpose masks (.npy)
param.help: 2D uint32 array where 0 = unassigned and [1..N] = cell index. Coordinates must be registered to transcript micron space. If not provided, ProSeg uses the prior cell assignments already in the transcript table (provided by the instrument).

param.name: cellpose_probs
param.label: Cellpose cell probabilities (.npy)
param.help: Optional float32 array matching the masks array, giving per-pixel segmentation confidence from Cellpose.

param.name: cellpose_scale
param.label: Cellpose scale (microns per pixel)
param.help: Scaling factor to convert Cellpose mask pixel coordinates to microns. Required when supplying Cellpose masks.

param.name: spaceranger_map
param.label: Space Ranger barcode mappings (Visium HD only)
param.help: barcode_mappings.parquet file from Space Ranger, providing prior segmentation for Visium HD data.

param.name: ignore_z
param.label: Ignore Z coordinate (force 2D)
param.help: Treat data as 2D even if a Z column is present. By default ProSeg models cells in 3D using voxel layers stacked on the Z axis.

param.name: compactness
param.label: Cell compactness
param.help: Smaller values enforce more compact (circular) cells. Larger values allow more elongated shapes.

param.name: connectivity
param.label: Enforce connectivity
param.help: Prevent cells from having disconnected voxel regions.

param.name: voxel_size
param.label: Voxel size (microns, X/Y)
param.help: Final voxel size in microns on the X/Y plane. Smaller = higher resolution, more memory. Must be an integer divisor of burnin voxel size.

param.name: burnin_voxel_size
param.label: Burnin voxel size (microns, X/Y)
param.help: Larger voxel size used during burn-in for faster initialisation. Must be an integer multiple of voxel size (e.g. 2.0 with voxel size 1.0).

param.name: voxel_layers
param.label: Voxel layers (Z axis)
param.help: Number of voxel layers stacked along the Z axis. Controls 3D resolution. Set to 1 to approximate 2D segmentation.

param.name: samples
param.label: Sampling iterations
param.help: Number of sampler iterations at full resolution.

param.name: burnin_samples
param.label: Burnin iterations
param.help: Number of preliminary sampler iterations at the larger burnin voxel size.

param.name: min_qv
param.label: Minimum quality value (QV)
param.help: Transcripts below this QV threshold are excluded. Requires a QV column to be specified. Default of 20 matches Xenium recommended filtering.

param.name: generate_polygons
param.label: Output 2D cell polygons (GeoJSON)
param.help: Consensus 2D polygons per cell, flattened from 3D segmentation.

param.name: polygon_layers
param.label: Output per-layer cell polygons (GeoJSON)
param.help: Separate non-overlapping polygon per Z layer, preserving 3D structure.

param.name: output_expected
param.label: Output expected counts (CSV)
param.help: Non-integer expected counts averaged over recorded samples.

param.name: output_cellmeta
param.label: Output cell metadata (CSV)
param.help: Cell centroids, volume, and summary statistics.

param.name: output_txmeta
param.label: Output transcript metadata (CSV)
param.help: Per-transcript revised positions, assignment probabilities, etc.

param.name: output_gene
param.label: Output gene metadata (CSV)
param.help: Per-gene summary statistics.

param.name: morph_steps
param.label: Morphology sub-iterations per sampling step
param.help: Number of cell morphology sampling sub-steps per main iteration.

param.name: ncomponents
param.label: Number of mixture components
param.help: Gene expression is modelled as a mixture of negative binomials. More components push cells toward distinct types; too many risks artefactual cell types.

param.name: diffusion_prob
param.label: Transcript diffusion probability
param.help: Prior probability that a transcript has diffused from its source cell.

param.name: recorded_samples
param.label: Recorded samples
param.help: Number of final-phase samples used to compute expectations and uncertainty estimates. Must be less than or equal to sampling iterations.

param.name: nuclear_reassign
param.label: Nuclear reassignment probability
param.help: Prior probability that the initial nuclear assignment is incorrect.

param.name: no_diffusion
param.label: Disable diffusion model
param.help: Disables transcript repositioning entirely. Useful if RNA leakage is not expected. Note: setting diffusion probability to 0.0 still allows small-scale repositioning; this flag disables it completely.

param.name: diff_sigma_near
param.label: Diffusion sigma (near)
param.help: Standard deviation for repositioning of non-diffused transcripts.

param.name: diff_sigma_far
param.label: Diffusion sigma (far)
param.help: Standard deviation for repositioning of diffused transcripts.

param.name: diff_sigma_z
param.label: Diffusion sigma (Z axis)
param.help: Standard deviation for transcript repositioning along the Z axis. Default is 0.1 microns.

tool.help:
ProSeg ======
ProSeg (probabilistic segmentation) is a Bayesian cell segmentation method for spatial transcriptomics. It models transcript-to-cell assignment as a probabilistic process, correcting for RNA diffusion, transcript misassignment, and imprecise prior segmentation.
The method is described in:
Jones et al. Cell simulation as cell segmentation. *Nat Methods* (2025). https://doi.org/10.1038/s41592-025-02697-0
Supported platforms -------------------
* **10x Xenium** -- CSV.GZ or Parquet transcript tables * **NanoString CosMx** -- pixel-coordinate or micron-coordinate tables * **Vizgen MERSCOPE / MERFISH** -- detected_transcripts table * **10x Visium HD** -- binned_outputs directory with Space Ranger barcodes * **AnnData** -- Zarr-encoded .h5ad input * **SpatialData** -- Zarr-encoded SpatialData input
How ProSeg works ----------------
ProSeg requires a prior segmentation -- either the cell/nucleus assignments already present in the transcript table (provided by the instrument), or explicit Cellpose masks registered to transcript space. It does **not** create new cells; it refines the boundaries of cells from the prior.
A Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) sampler iteratively reassigns transcripts to cells using a voxelised 3D grid. The burn-in phase runs at a coarser voxel resolution for speed; the final phase uses the specified voxel size.
3D segmentation ---------------
By default, ProSeg segments cells in **3D** using voxel layers stacked on the Z axis (controlled by ``Voxel layers``). To force 2D analysis, enable **Ignore Z coordinate**.
Outputs -------
The primary output is a **SpatialData Zarr archive** (tar-wrapped) readable in Python with::
import spatialdata sdata = spatialdata.read_zarr("proseg-output.zarr")
It contains:
* ``sdata.points["transcripts"]`` -- revised transcript positions * ``sdata.shapes["cell_boundaries"]`` -- 2D cell polygons * ``sdata.tables["table"]`` -- AnnData with count matrix, cell metadata, and spatial coordinates
To export to h5ad for use in R::
sdata.tables["table"].write_h5ad("proseg-anndata.h5ad")
Optional CSV outputs (counts, expected counts, cell metadata, transcript metadata, gene metadata) and GeoJSON polygon files can also be enabled.
Links -----
* GitHub: https://github.com/dcjones/proseg * Bioconda: https://anaconda.org/bioconda/rust-proseg * Paper: https://doi.org/10.1038/s41592-025-02697-0
